title: hieracium prenanthoides subsp. perfoliatum
intro: Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. perfoliatum, also known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) with yellow flowers and a distinctive leaf arrangement. The medicinal properties of Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. perfoliatum include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. perfoliatum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
